pi_items.source: ITIL v4 specification embedded in setup_task.sh (lines 23-28): End User pool and Service Desk pool
ITIL v4 specification embedded in setup_task.sh (lines 25-28): L1 Support, L2 Support, Incident Manager

ITIL v4 specification process flow (setup_task.sh lines 32-62): Log Incident, Categorize & Prioritize, Apply Known Fix, Investigate & Diagnose, Implement Resolution, Escalate to Major Incident, Close Incident
ITIL v4 specification process flow (setup_task.sh lines 30-62): Known Error?, L1 Resolvable?, Major Incident?, Resolved?
ITIL v4 specification (setup_task.sh): Start Event 'Incident Reported' (line 33), Timer Event 'SLA Breach Warning' (line 48), End Event 'Incident Closed' (line 62)

All keywords verified present in ITIL v4 specification embedded in setup_task.sh: 'incident' in process names, 'support' in L1 Support/L2 Support, 'l1'/'l2' in lane names, 'manager' in Incident Manager, 'resolve' in resolution tasks, 'diagnose' in Investigate & Diagnose, 'sla' in SLA Breach Warning, 'close' in Close Incident

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a BPMN 2.0 diagram for ITIL v4 Incident Management workflow with 2 pools (End User and Service Desk), 3 lanes (L1 Support, L2 Support, Incident Manager), 7 tasks (Log Incident, Categorize & Prioritize, Apply Known Fix, Investigate & Diagnose, Implement Resolution, Escalate to Major Incident, Close Incident), 4 gateways (Known Error, L1 Resolvable, Major Incident, Resolved), 3 events (Incident Reported start event, SLA Breach Warning timer event, Incident Closed end event), and must include all required keywords related to incident management processes. The specification file is synthetically generated during task setup and defines the complete ITIL incident management workflow including message flows between End User and Service Desk pools.
